items.title: Want a $1 Million Portfolio by Retirement? Here's How Much You Should Invest Every Month.
items.author: David Jagielski
items.description: To reach a $1 million portfolio by retirement, the article suggests investing in an ETF that tracks the S&P 500 or a growth-focused ETF, and provides estimates on the monthly investment required based on different annual return assumptions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article recommends the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust as a conventional option for risk-averse investors, as it tracks the S&P 500 index which has historically averaged a 10% annual return.
The article suggests the Vanguard Growth Index Fund ETF as a solid option for investors who want to focus more on growth stocks, which can have higher upside potential in the long run.
